articles.title: Mediabunny: A Complete Media Toolkit for JavaScript
articles.description: Supporting both browsers and Node.js, this library lets you read, write and convert popular media file formats (e.g. MP4, MP3, and more) without needing to lean on dependencies like FFmpeg. You can make thumbnails, extract metadata, write code that gets converted into a video, and more. GitHub repo.
articles.author: Vanilagy

articles.title: Why Do Browsers Throttle JavaScript Timers?
articles.description: Did you know the zero in setTimeout(0) doesn’t really mean zero? Browsers clamp timers to at least a few milliseconds or more, slowing down code that relies on them. Luckily, there are alternative options, which Nolan demonstrates and benchmarks.
articles.author: Nolan Lawson

articles.title: Ripple: A New, In-Development TypeScript UI Framework
articles.description: A new framework aiming to take the best parts of React, Solid and Svelte, and combine them into a single package. Notably, it comes from someone who worked on React and Svelte, as well as author of Lexical and Inferno, so it’s worth a look. There’s more info on the GitHub repo.
articles.author: Dominic Gannaway

articles.title: Google Chrome Turns 17: A History
articles.description: A fantastic walkthrough of Chrome’s origins and its evolution over the years. Addy looks at key milestones (multi-process architecture for example), security, its steps into the world of AI, and more.
articles.author: Addy Osmani

articles.title: Lean for JavaScript Developers
articles.description: A comprehensive introduction to Lean, a theorem prover and language for creating formally verified code that Dan Abramov has really been getting into recently. “I’ve never written code alongside proofs like this before. Have you?”
articles.author: Dan Abramov

articles.title: Getting Accurate Text Lengths with Intl.Segmenter
articles.description: A useful tip for when .length just isn’t returning what you’d quite expect..
articles.author: Sangeeth Sudheer

articles.title: Making XML Human-Readable Without XSLT
articles.description: Jake gets niche, but sets JavaScript to good use here.
articles.author: Jake Archibald

articles.title: Peaks.js 4.0: UI Component for Interacting with Audio Waveforms
articles.description: A project originally spawned from the BBC’s R&D department, Peaks renders audio waveforms to a canvas element and allows scrolling, zooming, and the sort of things you might otherwise see in an audio editor. LGPL licensed.
articles.author: Chris Needham et al.

articles.title: Redux Toolkit 2.9: The 'Batteries-Included' Toolset for Redux
articles.description: The long-standing toolset for  working with Redux for state management gets a performance update with a rewrite of RTK Query’s subscription and polling systems, automatic aborting of in-flight requests when a cache entry is removed, and more.
articles.author: Mark Erikson

articles.title: react-window 2.0: Render Large Lists of Data Quickly
articles.description: A component library for rendering large lists of data quickly and without the performance problems that often go along with that. There are several examples to check out, and React 18 is now required. (Tanstack Virtual is another option to consider in this space.)
articles.author: Brian Vaughn

articles.description: The What's New in Viteland: August 2025 recap presents a handy roundup of what's going on with Oxlint, Vite, Vitest, Rolldown, and related projects.
The Angular team has a big summer update too, covering zoneless change detection, new animation features, AI integrations, better debugging, and more.
Thoughtbot shares its list of upcoming JavaScript conferences for 2025.
Deno's Fresh framework is now out in Fresh 2.0 Beta form. It can now optionally run as a Vite plugin which opens up numerous new possibilities.
Electron 38.0 – The cross-platform desktop app framework. Now using Chromium 140 and Node 22.18 with V8 14.0.
Ember 6.6 – The framework "for building ambitious web applications."
Node.js v20.19.5 (LTS)

articles.title: ⚓️ The Basics of Anchor Positioning
articles.description: Ahmad runs us through the basics of Anchor Positioning (placing an element based on the position of another element). He highlights the problem it’s looking to solve, along with how this feature will work. All told with plenty of code and excellent visual examples. Browser support isn’t universal just yet, but it’s worth learning as it’s going to be super useful.
articles.author: Ahmad Shadeed

articles.title: Google Chrome at 17 Years Old: A History
articles.description: An amazingly thorough walkthrough here of Chrome’s origins, notable rise, and evolution over the years. Addy looks at various key milestones (multi-process architecture for example), and shares quite a few behind-the-scenes browser tidbits too. A good read.
articles.author: Addy Osmani

articles.title: Getting Creative with Images in Long-Form Content
articles.description: This is a fun article looking at a handful of ways in which we can, via images, break out from a typical rectilinear design to one that’s unique and eye-catching using just a few lines of CSS.
articles.author: Andy Clarke

articles.title: ▶  Visualizing Responsive Typography
articles.description: An overview of the various ways in which we can create fluid font sizes, generally expressed as a clamp() function, combining em (or rem) with vw (or vi) units. Miriam notes the results can often be difficult to parse and aims to help us wrap our heads around things.
articles.author: Miriam Suzanne

articles.title: You No Longer Need JavaScript (For Many Frontend Things)
articles.description: A neat look at some of what plain HTML and CSS can achieve in terms of effects and techniques you might once have relied upon JavaScript.
articles.author: Lyra

articles.title: Say Bye with JavaScript Beacon
articles.description: The Beacon API has been supported in all mainstream browsers for years now and provides a way to send a guaranteed (even if a page is being unloaded) non-blocking request to a web server when no response is needed.
articles.author: HemathKumar R

articles.title: CSS Elevator: A Pure CSS State Machine with Floor Navigation
articles.description: A fun look at how we can use features such as has(), custom properties, counters, and more to create something functional and interactive, without the need for JavaScript.
articles.author: Christian Sabourin

articles.title: How AI Is Changing Search Behaviors
articles.description: Generative AI isn’t replacing search, but it is reshaping it, becoming a growing part of how people now look for information.
articles.author: Moran, Rosala, Brown (NNg)

articles.title: Should You Preload Fonts for Performance?
articles.description: When preloading helps, when it hurts, and the rules to follow.
articles.author: Erwin Hofman

articles.title: Speeding Up Firefox Local AI Runtime
articles.description: How Firefox accelerated inference by replacing the WASM-based ONNX runtime that powers Transformers.js with a native C++ counterpart.
articles.author: Ziadé, Adenot, and Guelton

articles.title: OverType: The Markdown Editor That's a Textarea
articles.description: Described as an “under-engineered” solution that has “perfect WYSIWYG alignment using an invisible textarea overlay technique”, with optional toolbar. Try some demos or view it on GitHub.
articles.author: David Miranda

articles.title: Obs.js: Context-Aware Web Performance for Everyone
articles.description: Uses the Navigator and Battery APIs to get contextual information about your users’ connection strength and battery status so you can adapt your site/app to the environment or collect the data for analysis.
articles.author: Harry Roberts

articles.title: 90's Cursor Effects
articles.description: This is a blast of nostalgia! A neat collection of throwback cursor effects but designed for the modern web (fast, efficient). The associated repo is here. Not new, but new to me, and cool enough to share.
articles.author: Tim Holman

articles.title: EmbedPDF: A PDF Viewer that Integrates with any JavaScript Project
articles.description: No dependencies, supports any framework, and has features like themes, annotations, redaction, search, smooth scrolling, and more – all available via a dev-friendly API. See the docs or try a live demo.
articles.author: EmbedPDF

articles.title: Cornerstone3D 4.0: Build Web-Based Medical Imaging Apps
articles.description: A set of JavaScript libraries to build things like this 3D CT scan viewer, PET-CT scan viewer, and much more besides. There’s a lot to this project, including plenty of tutorials.
articles.author: Open Health Imaging Foundation
